comments: The very first FRS, which took over Friday nights from John Peel's show.

Start of show: "Hello, I'm Tommy Vance, and welcome to edition one of the Friday Rock Show, a programme that will in future weeks present the very best rock music that we can find, both past and present. Now tonight, you're in for a treat. Back in October, producer Tony Wilson my producer took a 24-track mobile down to Wembley and recorded Yes in concert. He then took the master tape into another studio, mixed it down from 24 tracks to two, specially for Radio 1 and for you. And here it is, uninterrupted: Yes in concert."

transcript.text: Hello, I'm Tommy Vance and welcome to edition one of the Friday Rock Show, a program that will in future weeks present the very best rock music that we can find, both past and present.
Now tonight, you're in for a treat. Back in October, Tony Wilson, my producer, took a 24-track mobile down to Wembley and recorded Yes In Concert.
He then took the master tape into another studio, mixed it down from 24 tracks to two, specially for Radio 1 and for you. And here it is, uninterrupted, Yes In Concert.
By the day, the circus of heaven came to town.
Waiting anxiously, we flocked inside with us 14 in months.

Yes, perform Siberian Kartru, Heart of the Sunrise, Circus from Heaven, Time and a Word, Long Distance Runaround, Fish, Perpetual Change, Soon, Don't Kill the Whale, The Clap, Starship Trooper, Freedom, Awaken, All Good People, and Roundabout.
The sound crew were Bill Aitken, Neil Byrne, and Nigel Luby.
Yes, In Concert at Wembley was produced by Tony Wilson.
And that was edition one of the Friday Rock Show.
I'll be back just after the 10 o'clock news next Friday.
It opens up again at 8 o'clock in the morning with Junior Choice and Ed Stewart.
We join Radio 2 after the time signal.
Wherever you happen to be in this world, have yourself a remarkable good night.
